title: Fix customer creation persistence bug - Frontend verification

requirements.summary: Verify the frontend CustomerForm component is correctly serializing and sending all customer data (name, phone, email, address) to the backend createCustomer mutation
acceptanceCriteria: Customer form data is properly formatted before sending to backend
All required fields are included in the mutation payload
Form validation prevents submission of incomplete data
Error handling displays specific error messages if backend returns failure

file_operations.description: Review and verify that the CustomerForm component correctly captures all four fields (name, phone, email, physicalAddress) and passes them to the useCreateCustomer mutation hook. Ensure form validation requires all fields before submission. Add or enhance error handling to display specific error messages from backend failures, including the 'Failed to add customer please try again' error. Verify the onSuccess callback properly triggers UI updates.

Review the useCreateCustomer mutation hook implementation to verify it correctly calls actor.createCustomer with all four parameters (name, phoneNumber, emailAddress, physicalAddress) in the correct order matching the backend interface. Ensure the mutation properly handles errors and returns meaningful error messages to the UI. Verify the mutation configuration includes proper error handling that can be displayed to users.

requirements.summary: Ensure the useCreateCustomer React Query mutation hook properly handles the backend response and updates the customer list cache after successful creation
acceptanceCriteria: React Query cache is invalidated or updated after successful customer creation
Newly created customer appears in the list without requiring page refresh
Loading states are properly managed during the creation process
Success and error states trigger appropriate UI feedback

file_operations.description: Verify the useCreateCustomer mutation includes an onSuccess handler that invalidates the customer list query cache using queryClient.invalidateQueries with the key ['customers']. Ensure this triggers an immediate refetch of getAllCustomers so the newly created customer appears in the list without page refresh. Verify loading states are properly exposed through the mutation's isLoading property.

Verify the CustomersPage component properly wires the CustomerForm's onSuccess callback to close the form dialog and display success feedback. Ensure the customer list automatically updates when the cache is invalidated by the mutation. Add toast notifications or success messages when customer creation succeeds. Verify error states from the mutation are displayed to users with appropriate error messages.

Ensure the CustomerForm component exposes mutation loading states to disable the submit button during creation and provides visual feedback. Verify the form resets after successful submission and displays error messages when the mutation fails. Ensure the form closes automatically on success via the onSuccess callback prop.
